title: Cleveland ICE Order of Supervision Lawyer | Brown Immigration
content:
## What is an Order of Supervision?
Have you recently been released from detention or custody of the United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agency? If so, then you may have been given an order of supervision upon your release. These orders are usually given by an immigration judge and require a person to periodically check in with immigration officers and abide by a very specific set of rules and standards. These orders are often given to someone who is awaiting a court hearing or final immigration order if they have been notified of removal and deportation proceedings against them.

### Reporting to Immigration Officers
Sometimes, these conditions can last months or years before any unanimous decisions are made pertaining to immigration or deportation status. During the waiting period, you will have to not only report to ICE periodically but also request advance permission to move, travel, leave the state, or do anything that may be construed – or misconstrued – as fleeing. If you feel the order of supervision is too strict or that you are being treated unfairly, then you may need to work with an attorney to help you adjust the order and file a motion with an immigration judge.

### Work with a Cleveland Immigration Lawyer at Brown Immigration Law
You deserve representation! If you wish to stay in the country, then it is important that you work with our firm. At Brown Immigration Law , we understand that this time in your life can be terrifying and the pressure to adhere to all the rules and regulations regarding your order of supervision can cause stress. With the experience of a dedicated Cleveland, OH immigration lawyer from our firm working for you, you will be able to procure a favorable outcome for your deportation or other immigration case. When you are feeling that the weight of the world is bearing down on your shoulders, do not hesitate to contact Brown Immigration Law .
